summarization.a: The 2θχ XRD pattern compares annealed 200cLi–MnO₂–600 with the unannealed sample. After annealing, clear LiMn₂O₄ reflections appear at ~18.7°, ~36–38°, and ~64–67°. These peaks are sharper and better defined than in the unannealed trace. A weak extra peak near ~48.6° is marked with an asterisk, indicating a minor impurity.
summarization.b: The θ–2θ scan highlights out-of-plane reflections for 200cLi–MnO₂ before and after annealing. The annealed sample shows strong LiMn₂O₄ peaks, along with labeled “lithiated phase” features near ~17° and ~35°. Residual MnO₂ peaks at ~22° and ~42° are still present. This shows that annealing promotes lithiated phase formation but does not fully remove MnO₂.
summarization.c: The 2θχ diffractograms compare annealed and unannealed 300cLi–MnO₂. In the annealed sample, LiMn₂O₄ peaks at (111), (131)/(222), (440), and (351) are clearly visible. These reflections are stronger and cleaner than in the unannealed pattern. This indicates improved crystallinity of the lithiated phase after annealing.
summarization.d: The θ–2θ patterns for 300cLi–MnO₂ show strong lithiated-phase peaks after annealing. LiMn₂O₄ reflections near ~18.7° and ~35.6° dominate the annealed trace. A small impurity peak, marked by an asterisk near ~33°, is also visible. The unannealed sample shows weaker and broader peaks, pointing to poorer structural order.

d.question: What does the dominance of LiMn₂O₄ reflections in the annealed θ–2θ pattern suggest about film structure?
d.answer: Dominant LiMn₂O₄ reflections indicate improved phase ordering along the surface-normal direction. This suggests that annealing enhances alignment or coherence of the lithiated phase. Such ordering is consistent with higher structural quality.

c.question: Compared with the unannealed sample, how do LiMn₂O₄ peaks change after annealing in subfigure (c)?
c.answer: They become stronger and more clearly resolved.

a.question: Why is the 2θχ geometry used to compare annealed and unannealed 200cLi–MnO₂ in this panel?
a.answer: The 2θχ geometry allows comparison of diffraction features that are less constrained by surface-normal orientation. This makes it suitable for assessing overall phase formation and crystallinity differences induced by annealing. It helps reveal sharpening and emergence of LiMn₂O₄ reflections.

b.question: Which features in subfigure (b) indicate formation of a lithiated phase after annealing?
